sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("Current white regions:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print("\nCurrent colored markers:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'N', 'p', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] < 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check ASCII around cols 6-57, rows 27-57
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nASCII rows 27-57, cols 4-58:")
for i in range(27, 58):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][4:58]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current state after toggling all three tracks:

- Initial: rows 52-57, px=52
- After toggle UP: rows 48-57, px=92

But earlier, when I toggled track 1 UP, the white region only expanded by 2 rows. Why did track 6 expand by 4 rows?

- Current white at cols 30-33: rows 56-57, px=8

So the white region at cols 38-47 absorbed 40 cells, and the white region at cols 28-35 lost 16 cells. But 40 ≠ 16, so the white regions didn't simply merge.
